v0: Initial Velocity: The velocity of the object at the moment it is thrown.
theta: Launch Angle: The angle at which the object is thrown relative to the horizontal.
h0: Initial Height: How high the object was from the ground at the time of launch.
R: Range: How far the object travels horizontally.
H: Maximum Height: The highest point the object reaches.
T: Time of Flight: The total time the object stays in the air.
g: Gravitational Acceleration: Acceleration downwards due to Earth's gravity (9.8 m/s²).
vx: Horizontal Component: The horizontal part of the velocity (remains constant).
vy: Vertical Component: The vertical part of the velocity (changes with time).
